refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR contained a vague term 'support achieving' which could imply implementation details. Refining it to 'enable achieving' focuses on the functional goal without prescribing how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single functional objective: meeting the energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it satisfies the customer-defined target (≤ E_target).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use
Design energy recovery mechanisms to reuse energy within the system
Establish a performance evaluation and reporting framework for energy efficiency
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Feedback control tightly linked to energy measurement accuracy
Operational parameters may influence multiple system aspects
coupling_check.notes: Selecting real-time monitoring and feedback control as DP supports modularity by isolating energy measurement and control functions, reducing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and reliable energy measurement is feasible within system constraints
Feedback control can be implemented without excessive complexity
Operational policies allow for adaptive control adjustments
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for performance evaluation and reporting to ensure continuous verification of energy efficiency targets and support operational decision-making.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the energy management and monitoring functionalities within the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive real-time energy consumption data and send feedback control signals.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with other system modules and external systems for data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and feedback control logically belong here; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Contains physical energy consumption but lacks direct control and monitoring logic, causing diffusion to other modules.
Primarily focused on remote control, not energy monitoring; poor containment.
Safety may interact with energy control but is not the primary owner; containment is weak.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control, as it centralizes energy management functions and minimizes diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause some coupling.
Interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link must be well-defined to avoid unintended diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target. The DP logically supports the measurement and control needed to meet the acceptance criteria of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause coupling and diffusion risk if interfaces are not tightly controlled.
Interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link must be well-defined to prevent unintended diffusion of DP responsibilities.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating good cohesion and minimal diffusion of the DP across IM boundaries. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through telemetry data and network communication interfaces that provide real-time energy consumption data and feedback control signals, enabling measurement against the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, as it centralizes energy management and monitoring functionalities, minimizing diffusion and supporting ownership clarity.

recommended_revisions: Define and enforce strict interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle module to minimize coupling and diffusion risk.
Clarify and document the roles of Telemetry and Network Link interfaces to ensure they support the DP without causing diffusion.
Consider monitoring and managing dependencies on external modules to maintain high containment and reduce diffusion over time.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirements with appropriate design parameters and implementation modules. The DP logically supports the FR, the IM boundary (NGHE Solution) shows high containment and low diffusion, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary further decomposition that could introduce complexity and diffusion risks. Instead, focus should be on enforcing recommended interface contracts and managing dependencies to maintain system integrity.

revise.why: To address identified diffusion risks by defining and enforcing strict interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle module, and clarifying roles of Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the FR acceptance criteria for energy efficiency.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate architectural boundary for implementing the DP.
Verification methods via telemetry and network interfaces are reliable and sufficient.
open_risks: Dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data may cause coupling and diffusion risk if interfaces are not tightly controlled.
Potential diffusion of DP responsibilities through Telemetry and Network Link interfaces if not well-defined.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle module.
Verification test results demonstrating real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control effectiveness.
Monitoring reports on dependency management and diffusion metrics over time.
